title: ARM Registers and Basic Instructions
summary: Learn the ARM register set and core instructions: MOV, ADD, SUB, MUL, and logical operations.
content_html:
ARM has 16 registers (R0–R15), each 32 bits wide. Some have special roles:

  • R0–R12: general purpose
  • R13 (SP): Stack Pointer
  • R14 (LR): Link Register (return address)
  • R15 (PC): Program Counter (next instruction address)

Basic Data Instructions

MOV R0, #5       ; R0 = 5  (immediate value)
MOV R1, R0       ; R1 = R0 (copy register)
ADD R2, R0, R1   ; R2 = R0 + R1
SUB R3, R2, #3   ; R3 = R2 - 3
MUL R4, R0, R1   ; R4 = R0 * R1

Logical Instructions

AND R0, R1, R2   ; R0 = R1 AND R2
ORR R0, R1, R2   ; R0 = R1 OR R2
EOR R0, R1, R2   ; R0 = R1 XOR R2
MVN R0, R1       ; R0 = NOT R1

Shift Instructions

LSL R0, R1, #2   ; Logical shift left 2 bits (multiply by 4)
LSR R0, R1, #1   ; Logical shift right 1 bit (divide by 2)

CPSR — Condition Flags

The Current Program Status Register stores flags after arithmetic: N (negative), Z (zero), C (carry), V (overflow). Suffix S updates flags: ADDS R0, R1, R2.

exercises.prompt: Which ARM register is the Program Counter?

exercises.title: MOV immediate
options: Loads the immediate value 10 into R0
exercises.answer: Loads the immediate value 10 into R0

exercises.title: ADD instruction
exercises.prompt: What does 'ADD R2, R0, R1' do?
options: Adds R0 and R1, stores in both

exercises.title: CPSR flags
exercises.prompt: Which flags does the CPSR store?
options: N, Z, C, V (Negative, Zero, Carry, Overflow)
exercises.answer: N, Z, C, V (Negative, Zero, Carry, Overflow)

exercises.title: S suffix
exercises.prompt: Adding the 'S' suffix to an instruction (e.g. ADDS) means:
options: Update the condition flags in CPSR
exercises.answer: Update the condition flags in CPSR
